evals.prompt: Convert the Spark UDF at input/array-segment-sum/ArraySegmentSumUDF.scala to a cuDF RapidsUDF and verify the GPU output matches the CPU UDF. A passing unit test is provided at input/array-segment-sum/UnitTest.scala.
evals.expected_output: A cuDF RapidsUDF whose GPU output matches the CPU UDF in schema and values on the unit-test data, implemented with pure columnar cuDF ops, running on the GPU with no CPU fallback and no memory leaks.
assertions: Implements a cuDF RapidsUDF reproducing every branch of the CPU UDF (including edge case handling and branch semantics) and preserving the result type
Keeps the GPU path pure columnar - no copyToHost or host-side per-row logic
Asserts the GPU output is identical in schema and values to the CPU UDF on the same unit-test data
Confirms the RapidsUDF executes on the GPU with no CPU fallback
Confirms there are no GPU memory leaks
Keeps the implementation general - no hardcoded test values or row-specific branches

evals.prompt: How do I increase the executor memory for my Spark job on YARN?
evals.expected_output: A one-line Spark cluster-config answer; not a request to convert a UDF, so udf-convert-to-cudf should not activate.
assertions: Does not load the udf-convert-to-cudf skill

evals.prompt: How do I read a Parquet file into a DataFrame in PySpark?
evals.expected_output: Basic Spark I/O question, unrelated to converting a UDF to a cuDF RapidsUDF; no UDF skill should activate.
assertions: Does not load the udf-convert-to-cudf skill
